strengths: 20 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency backend systems, exceeding the 15-year requirement
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map, plus early-stage startup experience (WatchUp, Apon)
Recent AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks, showing builder-level cloud/AI fluency
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML depth or recognized AI expertise — no shipped AI products at scale, publications, or ML systems experience beyond recent agent tooling
Career trajectory is senior/staff IC on product teams; no evidence of director-level scope, cross-org technical strategy, or advising strategic customers/partners
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; public speaking record (FOSS4G, testing workshops) is niche rather than the industry-level thought leadership the role demands

key_factors.factor: AI strategy and recognized AI expertise
Experience tenure and entrepreneurial builder profile

response.justification: The candidate clearly meets the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science (MIT) and roughly 20 years of engineering experience spanning TypeScript/Node product work, low-latency DLang microservices at Dunnhumby, and founding GISCollective — solid evidence of builder fluency and entrepreneurship. However, the role's core competencies are AI strategy leadership, recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI-built products, and director-level influence over product direction and strategic customers. The résumé shows only recent, tooling-level AI exposure (MCP generators at re:cinq since 2025), no ML systems or AI product ownership, no advanced AI degree, and titles that top out at Staff/Senior IC without evidence of org-wide strategic scope. Strong senior engineer, but a poor match for a director-level AI strategy principal role.
